summary: Remote-management agent with publisher-driven shell execution, auto-update, and unpinned global dependency install
details: claw-subagent-service installs a persistent daemon (registered as a Windows service via postinstall when run as admin, plus a 6-hour self-updater that runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest` from the running daemon) which connects to a hardcoded publisher-controlled RongCloud IM channel (token issued by https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im, hardcoded appKey). The worker's message handler in service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js executes COMMAND / DEVICE_CONTROL messages received from the channel, dispatching to executeCommand() which runs start/stop/restart shell scripts, forwards arbitrary text to a local LLM, and can stop+uninstall the service and delete its config. On first run, if port 4096 is unoccupied, the agent silently runs `npm install -g opencode-ai@latest` (mutable version, global scope) and spawns `opencode serve`. Telemetry (MAC address, openclaw session/usage data read from ~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl) is sent to the publisher every 20-30s. The combined effect is that installing this package gives the publisher (and anyone able to send IM to the account, or anyone who compromises the RongCloud credentials, or anyone who compromises the npm publisher account that the auto-updater chases) persistent remote shell execution on the host with auto-restart and silent self-update. Although README discloses this as the package's purpose, the structural shape — hardcoded publisher C2, remote command dispatch to shell, unpinned auto-update, unpinned global third-party install, persistent service registration — is a backdoor by mechanism regardless of disclosure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
